title: allosorus coriaceus
intro: Allosorus coriaceus, also known as the prickly spleenwort, is a type of fern native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its distinctive, leathery fronds with sharp, prickly margins. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Allosorus coriaceus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic agent to prevent infection. Allosorus coriaceus can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in herbal remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
